notes: Codex CLI enabled for: blackboxer using codex.
Claude CLI enabled for: ghostwire, ironclad using claude.
Gemini CLI enabled for: cinder using gemini.
Each corner ran against a real fixture workspace in a fresh temp directory.
Saved fight captures include transcript snippets plus stdout and stderr tails when the adapter exposes them.
Any unselected fighters still use the built-in scripted adapters so you can mix real and deterministic corners.

agents.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
agents.tagline: Smallest diff in the room, highest trust at the table.

agents.name: Claude Opus 4.6
agents.tagline: Wins ugly, ships clean, sleeps at night.

agents.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
agents.tagline: Explosive upside, occasional scene of the crime.

agents.name: GPT-5.4 Mini
agents.tagline: Treats every match like a profiler trace.

tasks.name: Checkout Guard
tasks.stakes: A coupon bug is quietly eating margin and trust.
tasks.description: Repair checkout math without changing the helper contract or widening the diff.
tasks.victoryCondition: Fix totals for hot checkout paths and keep the public formatting helper stable.

tasks.name: Session Shield
tasks.stakes: Client payloads are exposing credentials and over-privileged scopes.
tasks.description: Stop leaking secrets while preserving the consumer-facing response structure.
tasks.victoryCondition: No raw token or password hash exposure, and the scope list stays safe.

tasks.name: Dedupe Dojo
tasks.stakes: A nightly dedupe stage is burning time and scrambling record order.
tasks.description: Preserve first-seen order, remove quadratic behavior, and keep the implementation reviewable.
tasks.victoryCondition: Correct ordering plus a materially faster hot path.

fights.headline: Claude Sonnet 4.6 vs Claude Opus 4.6

keyMoments: Claude Sonnet 4.6 landed the cleaner review line.
Claude Opus 4.6 kept the fight close on execution.
Percent coupon should only affect subtotal, not shipping.

transcript.title: Corner summary
transcript.text:
direct hotfix
Line 10: removed `* (1 - coupon.value)` from shipping term so percent coupons discount only the subtotal, not shipping. One-line change; no signature or export changes.
Notable move: Identified that `shipping * (1 - coupon.value)` was the margin-eating bug and fixed it to just `shipping` in the percent-coupon branch.

transcript.title: Corner summary
transcript.text:
direct
Removed raw `passwordHash` and `token` from response object. Masked token to show only last 4 chars. Added scope allowlist filter and deduplication to prevent privilege escalation via extraScopes.
Notable move: Scope allowlist with Set-based filtering — blocks arbitrary scope injection from user.extraScopes while keeping the export contract intact.

transcript.title: Workspace diff
transcript.text: session.mjs changed across 27 touched lines.

transcript.title: Fixture verdict
transcript.text: Full session tokens must never be returned to the client payload.

workspaceNotes: ALLOWED_SCOPES is a reasonable default but may need tuning to match the actual permission model — add or remove entries as needed.
If callers relied on the top-level `token` or `passwordHash` fields, they will break — intentionally, since those were the leak.
Full session tokens must never be returned to the client payload.
Privilege scopes should be deduped and stripped down to a safe allowlist.

blue.diffSummary: Removed raw `passwordHash` and `token` from response object. Masked token to show only last 4 chars. Added scope allowlist filter and deduplication to prevent privilege escalation via extraScopes.
blue.notableMove: Scope allowlist with Set-based filtering — blocks arbitrary scope injection from user.extraScopes while keeping the export contract intact.

transcript.title: Corner summary
transcript.text:
direct
Replaced O(n^2) nested-loop duplicate check with a Set for O(n) lookups. Removed the trailing .sort() that was scrambling first-seen order.
Notable move: Swapped the inner linear scan for Set.has(), cutting the hot path from O(n^2) to O(n) while preserving insertion order.

blue.diffSummary: Replaced O(n^2) nested-loop duplicate check with a Set for O(n) lookups. Removed the trailing .sort() that was scrambling first-seen order.
blue.notableMove: Swapped the inner linear scan for Set.has(), cutting the hot path from O(n^2) to O(n) while preserving insertion order.

red.promptStyle: I have replaced the quadratic deduplication algorithm with a linear $O(n)$ strategy using a `Set` for record ID tracking. I also removed the post-deduplication sorting step, which preserves the original first-seen order of the records as required.
